title: Apply for a CARICOM–Dominican Republic certificate of origin
description: Get a certificate of origin under the CARICOM–Dominican Republic Free Trade Agreement.
summary: The Free Trade Agreement between CARICOM and the Dominican Republic gives qualifying goods preferential customs treatment. The Certificate of Origin is the proof.
The form is bilingual – the same fields appear in English and in Spanish, so the certificate is recognised on both sides of the agreement. The Spanish heading is ‘Certificado de Origen’.
You will need to confirm the country of origin, the HS code, the value, the quantity, and how the goods qualify under the rules of origin. BIDC reviews the application and issues the certificate.
If you are exporting regularly under this agreement, keep good records – the Dominican Republic customs authority can ask for evidence supporting any certificate.

items: the importer or consignee’s name and address
the goods description, HS code, value, quantity and country of origin

items.name: Bill of lading or airway bill
Proof that the goods meet the CARICOM–Dominican Republic rules of origin

how_long: Certificates are usually issued within 3 to 5 working days.

options.heading: In person
content: Take the completed CARICOM–Dominican Republic certificate of origin (Certificado de Origen) to BIDC at Pelican House.

contact.address:
Pelican House, Princess Alice Highway
St. Michael

assumptions: ‘Certificates are usually issued within 3 to 5 working days’ is an estimate.
